# UC-21.8.13 · Asterisk Queue Log Abandon Rate and Service Level Breach

## Description

The Asterisk `queue_log` is the canonical source for ENTERQUEUE, ABANDON, and CONNECT events. Hourly abandon percentage and connect ratio highlight understaffed intervals or misrouted IVR paths before executive dashboards show revenue impact.

## Value

Protects revenue and NPS on carrier or enterprise contact centers running Asterisk queues by exposing abandon spikes within an hour.

## Implementation

Monitor `/var/log/asterisk/queue_log` with correct line breaking (each line is one event). Extract `event`, `qname`/`queue`, and hold time fields if present on ABANDON. Tune thresholds: retail often tolerates higher abandons than emergency services. Join with workforce management schedules via lookup on `q`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• `props.conf` `LINE_BREAKER` for queue_log pipe format.
• Clock sync between Asterisk and Splunk.

Step 1 — Validate event counts against `queue show stats`.

Step 2 — Exclude marketing test campaigns via ANI lookup.

Step 3 — Alert routing to WFM team with queue name.

Step 4 — Archive raw queue_log for regulatory retention if required.

## SPL

```spl
index=voip sourcetype=asterisk:queue_log
| eval q=coalesce(queue, qname)
| eval is_abandon=if(event="ABANDON",1,0), is_enter=if(event="ENTERQUEUE",1,0), is_answer=if(event="CONNECT",1,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(is_enter) as enters, sum(is_abandon) as abandons, sum(is_answer) as answers by _time, host, q
| eval abandon_rate=round(100*abandons/nullif(enters,0),2)
| eval sla_met=round(100*answers/nullif(enters,0),2)
| where enters>30 AND (abandon_rate>15 OR sla_met<75)
```

## Visualization

Line chart (abandon_rate, sla_met), Bar chart (worst queues), Heatmap (hour × queue).

## References

- [Asterisk Documentation — Queue logs](https://docs.asterisk.org/Operation/Logging/Queue-Logs/)
